bootstrap_exception.description: Bootstrap implementation exceptions are process-local only and must be explicitly enabled by CLI flag.

blocked_rules.summary: Legacy mounted launchers cannot become the primary runtime.
Legacy Linux, mounted-launcher, or remote-route paths cannot be reintroduced.
Generated config, wrapper, and mirror files must not be manually edited.
Generated mirrors must never be used as render input, user override input, or authority input.
Stale active config flags such as telepathy are blocked; trusted Windows full-access posture is allowed by policy.
Normal code modification requests are blocked while Serena startup activation is incomplete.
Protected changes require actual Context7 evidence.
Unrelated dirty changes must not be reverted, reformatted, or cleaned up.
Destructive or system-changing commands remain blocked.
Sandbox and approval posture must match the Windows-native authority defaults.
Instructions must not force mixed Windows and legacy Linux Git execution.
Projectless chat or app state cannot authorize code modification without a repo root.
Codex App memory, context, or restore state can hint but cannot authorize changes.
Hooks may trigger checks, but hooks alone cannot be claimed as the final enforcement boundary.
Plugin-provided MCP or config drift must be audited before code modification.

warning_rules.summary: Large refactors without explicit scope are warned.
Implementation requests without explicit verification guidance are warned.
Mixed app, CLI, IDE, or runtime surfaces are warned.
Local and canonical remote runtime mismatches are warned or blocked depending on scope.
Changes near protected files are warned even when no protected diff is detected.
Git surface drift that does not directly block the current task is warned.

required_reminders.runtime_authority_conflict: runtime authority conflict: Codex App must remain Windows-native local; legacy Linux/remote execution is decommissioned.
required_reminders.serena_first_unmet: Serena-first is unmet: activate the canonical project and complete onboarding before normal code changes.
required_reminders.context7_missing: Context7 evidence is missing for a protected change.
required_reminders.unrelated_dirty_present: Unrelated dirty changes exist and must remain untouched.
required_reminders.destructive_blocked: Destructive or system-changing commands require explicit user authorization and verified target paths.
required_reminders.sandbox_blocked: Sandbox or approval posture changes must match Windows-native authority.
required_reminders.generated_mirror_blocked: Generated mirrors are outputs only and cannot be treated as authority or override sources.
required_reminders.hooks_trigger_only: Hooks may trigger checks, but audit, tests, and score layer remain the final gates.
